title: Herby Grilled Halloumi with Cherry Salsa
summary: Tangy cherry salsa meets salty golden halloumi in this super fresh herb-packed summer plate. It’s bold, bright and ridiculously moreish.

method.description: Split the halloumi along its natural crease, then chop into 2cm cubes. If using fresh cherries, halve and pit them. Set aside.
Pick the mint leaves and place them in a bowl of cold water. Finely chop the parsley and coriander, including the stalks. Quarter the cucumber lengthways, then finely dice.

Add the chopped herbs and cucumber to a bowl and set aside. Add the cherries to a separate bowl. Stir in the pomegranate molasses, sumac and honey. Squeeze in the lemon juice and mix. Taste and adjust with a little more honey if needed – it should be sweet and sour. Set aside.

Pour the water and a splash of olive oil into a saucepan. Set over a medium-high heat and bring to the boil. Once boiling, stir in the bulgur wheat, let it return to the boil, then cover and reduce the heat to low. Cook for 10 mins until the liquid is absorbed.
Remove from the heat and fluff up the bulgur with a fork. Squeeze over the remaining lemon juice and season with salt. Let it cool for a few mins, then stir through the cucumber herb mix.
Set a frying pan over a medium heat with a drizzle of olive oil. Once hot, fry the halloumi pieces for 5–7 mins until golden on all sides.

Divide the herby bulgur between plates and top with a dollop of yoghurt, the grilled halloumi, and a generous spoonful of the cherry salsa. Finish with a drizzle of olive oil and scatter over the mint leaves.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
